key_insight: The paper argues that LLM agents need an internal world model—trained to simulate future states and estimate plan quality—to move from reactive responses to genuinely foresightful long-horizon planning.
engineering_relevance: For agent builders, this suggests a practical training recipe for improving planning reliability: first inject predictive capability, then teach the output format, then calibrate the foresight signal with reinforcement learning.
method: WM-AMT: mid-train the policy on agentic look-ahead traces to build latent predictive capability.
FE-SFT: fine-tune the model to verbalize future rollouts and success estimates in a consistent structured format.
FC-RL: reinforce calibration and usefulness of the generated simulations and plan-conditioned value estimates.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports consistent gains over other training baselines on search and mathematical reasoning tasks.
watch_item: A key implementation risk is the format-capability gap: if the model is only trained to imitate look-ahead text, it may sound foresighted without actually grounding predictions.
paper_title: Internalizing the Future: A Unified Agentic Training Paradigm for World Model Planning
